repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest pid_synth_control_factor_misspec_v1 is scalar factor-misspecification partial ID, and panel_sdid_mc_id_v1 is mean estimator span/equality

published_prior_art.cite: Gunsilius2023DiSCo gives a unique weighted donor-quantile object, not a finite-donor monotone compatibility sharp set

repo_prior_art.cite: none; adjacent catalogue entries do not target full counterfactual quantile/CDF identification

published_prior_art.cite: Gunsilius2023DiSCo is closest but uses distance preservation / weighted quantile workflow, not this augmented affine row-span boundary

repo_prior_art.cite: none; pid_synth_control_factor_misspec_v1 is scalar Chebyshev misspecification, not distributional rank-factor escape

published_prior_art.cite: Chen2020DistributionalSC and FermanPinto2021ImperfectFit motivate matching/fit failures but do not give open-set exact-prefit rank-factor nonidentification
Gunsilius2023DiSCo already supplies the weighted donor-quantile corner; this theorem repackages it as a stable-loading sufficient condition

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The statement concludes existence of monotone rank-invariant interactive quantile-factor laws but omits Assumptions ass:monotone and ass:qif from its hypothesis list; add them explicitly.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Patch Conjecture 2 to include monotone quantile regularity and rank-invariant interactive quantile factors explicitly in the assumptions.
Add a concrete smallest-case witness for the open-set escape, e.g. J=3 with one affine null direction, to make the relative-open nonidentification kernel less schematic.
Correct the Masini bibliography metadata to the verified Journal of Econometrics listing, vol. 249(PA), article 105675, DOI 10.1016/j.jeconom.2024.105675.

recommended_next_step: Revise the Conjecture 2 hypothesis list, then rerun Stage -0.5; the novelty kernel survives on both repo and published axes.
